description_long: This object is a banana peel with three distinct, elongated flaps splayed outward, creating a tripod-like structure. The peel is a pale yellow color, with a slightly matte finish and subtle surface texture, typical of a banana skin. The stem is intact and protrudes upward from the center, while the interior of the peel is visible along the edges of each flap. There are no visible blemishes, tears, or bruises on the surface, and the peel appears to be in good condition. The object is lightweight and thin, with a flexible, semi-soft surface. Its form and color make it easily recognizable as a discarded banana peel, commonly found on urban sidewalks or streets.
description: A pale yellow banana peel with three splayed flaps and an intact stem, showing a clean, matte surface and no visible blemishes.
description_view_0: Front view showing the banana peel's three flaps spread wide, with the stem centered and upright.
description_view_1: Angled side view highlighting the curvature of the flaps and the upward tilt of the stem.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the broadest flap facing forward and the other two flaps extending to the sides.
description_view_3: Opposite angled side view, emphasizing the separation between the flaps and the stem's position.
attribute_peel_condition: The banana peel is intact with no visible bruising, tears, or splits. The surface appears smooth and unblemished, with a consistent texture throughout.
